text: You have three hours (plus any pre-agreed individual adjustment) to complete the paper.
Answer four essay questions, TWO from Section A and TWO from Section B.
Each essay question carries the same number of marks.
Candidates are advised to devote equal time to each question.

This is a closed book exam.
Candidates should not copy and paste images from any other documents.
Where appropriate (e.g. for formulae, equations, or diagrams) you may hand write or draw on the Inspera paper provided.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer TWO questions from SECTION A and TWO questions from SECTION B.
content: Discuss how B cell receptor (BCR) diversity is generated both before and after encounter with antigen.
Compare and contrast the adaptive immune response to intracellular and extracellular pathogens.
Using examples, describe how viruses modify host cells to benefit the virus.
Discuss mechanisms of virus transmission and intervention strategies to prevent virus spread between hosts.
Why can different degrees of disease severity be observed in infections caused by the same parasite? Use a range of parasites to illustrate your answer.
How do bacteria that cause chronic infections colonise and survive in mammalian hosts?
How does ischaemia damage cells and how is the damage reflected in morphological and functional changes of cells and tissues?
Discuss the hallmarks of cancer and their underlying molecular mechanisms.
